name: TCFD Climate Scenario Parameters (SSP-based)
description: Standard climate scenario definitions aligned with IPCC AR6 SSP pathways for TCFD scenario analysis. Includes temperature pathways, carbon price trajectories, energy mix assumptions, physical risk parameters, and transition risk parameters.
source: IPCC AR6 WG1/WG3, NGFS Phase IV, IEA World Energy Outlook 2023

scenarios.name: SSP1-1.9 (1.5°C aligned)
scenarios.description: Very low GHG emissions pathway. CO2 emissions cut to net zero around 2050. Consistent with limiting warming to 1.5°C with no or limited overshoot.

temperaturePathway.notes: Best estimate peak warming ~1.5°C in 2040s, declining to ~1.4°C by 2100 (AR6 WG1 Table SPM.1)

carbonPriceTrajectory_USD_tCO2.notes: Based on NGFS Net Zero 2050 scenario carbon price pathway. Prices in 2020 USD.

energyMix2050.notes: IEA Net Zero Emissions scenario primary energy shares by 2050

seaLevelRise_cm.notes: AR6 WG1 Table 9.9, median and likely range

extremeHeatDays_increase.notes: Additional days per year exceeding 35°C relative to 1995-2014 baseline (AR6 WG1 Ch 12)

precipitationChange_pct.notes: Annual mean precipitation change (%) at 1.5°C warming relative to 1850-1900 (AR6 WG1 Ch 8)

strandedAssetProbability.notes: Probability of significant asset value loss by 2050 under this pathway

technologyDisruptionTimeline.notes: Approximate year of major disruption milestone

scenarios.name: SSP1-2.6 (2°C aligned)
scenarios.description: Low GHG emissions pathway. CO2 emissions declining to net zero after 2050. Consistent with limiting warming to below 2°C.

temperaturePathway.notes: Best estimate warming reaches ~1.8°C by 2100 (AR6 WG1 Table SPM.1)

carbonPriceTrajectory_USD_tCO2.notes: Based on NGFS Below 2°C scenario. Prices in 2020 USD.

energyMix2050.notes: Primary energy shares consistent with SSP1-2.6 marker scenario

seaLevelRise_cm.notes: AR6 WG1 Table 9.9, median and likely range

extremeHeatDays_increase.notes: Additional days per year exceeding 35°C relative to 1995-2014 baseline

precipitationChange_pct.notes: Annual mean precipitation change (%) at ~2°C warming

strandedAssetProbability.notes: Probability of significant asset value loss by 2050

technologyDisruptionTimeline.notes: Approximate year of major disruption milestone

scenarios.name: SSP2-4.5 (Moderate / Middle of the Road)
scenarios.description: Intermediate GHG emissions pathway. CO2 emissions remain around current levels until mid-century, then declining. Warming of ~2.7°C by 2100.

temperaturePathway.notes: Best estimate warming ~2.7°C by 2100 (AR6 WG1 Table SPM.1)

carbonPriceTrajectory_USD_tCO2.notes: Moderate policy ambition. Based on NGFS NDCs scenario trajectory.

energyMix2050.notes: Primary energy shares under middle-of-the-road assumptions

seaLevelRise_cm.notes: AR6 WG1 Table 9.9, median and likely range

extremeHeatDays_increase.notes: Additional days per year exceeding 35°C relative to 1995-2014 baseline

precipitationChange_pct.notes: Annual mean precipitation change (%) at ~2.7°C warming

strandedAssetProbability.notes: Probability of significant asset value loss by 2050

technologyDisruptionTimeline.notes: Approximate year of major disruption milestone

scenarios.name: SSP5-8.5 (Very High Emissions / 4°C+)
scenarios.description: Very high GHG emissions pathway. CO2 emissions roughly double by 2050. Fossil-fueled development. Warming of ~4.4°C by 2100.

temperaturePathway.notes: Best estimate warming ~4.4°C by 2100 (AR6 WG1 Table SPM.1). Very likely range 3.3-5.7°C.

carbonPriceTrajectory_USD_tCO2.notes: Current Policies scenario. Minimal carbon pricing. Prices in 2020 USD.

energyMix2050.notes: Primary energy shares under fossil-fueled development assumptions

seaLevelRise_cm.notes: AR6 WG1 Table 9.9, median and likely range. Ice-sheet instability could add substantially.

extremeHeatDays_increase.notes: Additional days per year exceeding 35°C relative to 1995-2014 baseline

precipitationChange_pct.notes: Annual mean precipitation change (%) at ~4.4°C warming

strandedAssetProbability.notes: Low transition risk but extreme physical risk. Stranded assets unlikely due to continued demand.

technologyDisruptionTimeline.notes: No policy-driven phase-outs. Market-driven transitions only.
